ety: dhasak˚ = Prk. धसक्क(इ), fr. S. ध्वंस+कृ
def: v.n. To give way, to slip (out of place); to sink (as a slough); to yield, to slip or fall down, give way (as a mud wall); to enter, pierce, stick (into); — to cough.
more: v. n. To give way, to sink (as, a quagmire or slough).

def: s.f.(m.?) A slough, &c. ( = dhasan, dhasān, q.v.). s.f. Name of a river which rises in the Vindhya hills, flows through Bundelkhanḍ, and falls into the Betwā (a tributary of the Jamnā).

ety: S. दशार्णा; but considered by some as identical with dhasān following
def: s.f. Name of a river which rises in the Vindhya hills, flows through Bundelkhanḍ, and falls into the Betwā (a tributary of the Jamnā).
more: (from دهسنا) s. m. A slough, bog, quagmire, a swamp.

def: s.f. The state of being thrust into; — adj. & s.f. = dhasān, q.v. s.f. Name of a river which rises in the Vindhya hills, flows through Bundelkhanḍ, and falls into the Betwā (a tributary of the Jamnā).
more: n. f. S. ध्वंसन A quagmire; slough; bog; swamp. dhasan, dhasān . adj. Swampy (ground).
(from دهسنا) s. f. 1. A quagmire. 2. The state of being thrust into.

def: ( = dhasan), adj. & s.f.(m.?) Having quicksands, swampy, boggy; — a quicksand, swamp, bog, slough, quagmire.
v.t. To cause to sink (into, -meṅ), to cause to enter or pierce, to thrust or drive (into), to stick (into)
more: Caus. of دهسنا Aṛ baṛ kailā to paṅkië meṅ dhaṅsā deb . Bhoj. If you give me any more jaw I'll just thrust you down the bog.
(causal of دهسنا) v. a. To cause to be thrust into, to sink.
v. a. To drive or thrust in.

def: s.m. A slough, &c. ( = dhasān, q.v.). s.f. Name of a river which rises in the Vindhya hills, flows through Bundelkhanḍ, and falls into the Betwā (a tributary of the Jamnā).
more: (from دهسنا) s. m. A slough, bog, quagmire, a swamp.

def: adj. Sunken.
s.m. (fr. dhasaknā, q.v.) = dhasak. s.f. Giving way, sinking; coughing, cough ( = dhāṅs).

ety: dhas˚ = Prk. धंस(इ) or धस(इ) = S. ध्वंम(ते), rt. ध्वंस्
def: v.n. To sink (into, -meṅ, as into a slough); to enter, run (into), stick (into), pierce; to be pierced, be stuck (into), be thrust (into); to yield, give way
more: v. n. 1. To give way; sink (in a quagmire, etc.). 1. Bachchā ke peṭ dhasal bā! Bhoj. The child's stomach is bent in. (It's empty). 2. Aisā dhap dūṅgā ki dhaṛ meṅ sir dhas jāëgā . I'll give you such a blow that I'll flatten your head for you. 3. Hamre hiye meṅ nand nadan dhaso, jag ke sab log haṅso so haṅso! Nand's son within my heart is set, the world may laugh, I care not that . 2. To enter; penetrate; pierce.
v. n. To be pierced, stuck into, to be penetrated, to be thrust into, to sink, to enter, run into.

def: s.m. A mineral, fossil, metal, ore, &c. (see dhātu); — s.f. Semen virile
more: n. f. S. धातु 1. Ore; metal; mineral; fossil. 2. A principle or humour of the body; an elementary substance or property. 3. Semen virile. Tātā, tītā, āmlā, tīnoṅ dhāt bināsh . Prov. Warm food, hot things, and sour, make the semen thin and unfit for procreation. dhāt-chhīn ; P. jiryān , n. f. Seminal weakness.
s. m. 1. Ore, metal, mineral. 2. The Sanskrit root. 3. s. f. Semen virile

def: s.f. A habit; a passion, mania; a bad habit, vice
more: n. f. S. धृत assumed. 1. A mania; passion. 2. A bad habit. dhat paṛnā , v. n. To be addicted to; to grow upon (a bad habit).
A word used to encourage elephants.
subs: dhat paṛnā. v.n. A habit to be acquired
or to grow (on one), to become addicted (to).

def: s.f. A word used to encourage elephants, or to stop them, &c.
subs: intj. Stop! stand! halt! lie down!

def: s.m. One who places or lays, a founder, maker, creator, originator, author; supporter, maintainer, preserver, sustainer, nourisher, parent; name of Brahmā; name of one of the Ádityas
subs: s.m. Name of Brahmā; name of two of the deities.

ety: prob. S. धर्तृ, धर्ता; rt. धृ
def: s.m. A handkerchief or cloth tied over the ears and head (to keep the beard up).
more: s. m. A handkerchief tied over the turband and over the ears.

def: n. m. H. ḍāḍhī beard. A piece of cloth tied under the beard to make the hairs incline upward.
s.m. The act of driving away; — putting off, evasion
more: n. m. The act of driving away. 2. Putting off; evasion. dhatā batānā , v. n. To put off; turn out ( ṭālnā ). Kisko muṅh lagā rakkhā hai? dhatā batāo!
subs: v.t. To drive away, turn out; to put off.

def: s.f. The tree Grislea tomentosa.
s.f. Binding tight, stretching on the rack; rack, torment, torture; — a noose or gin fastened on a horse's nose; nose-string (of an ox, or camel, &c.; syn. nakel)
more: s. f. 1. Rack, torment, torture. 2. A noose or gin fastened on a horseʼs nose.
subs: or denā, or lagānā (-kī, or -ko), To fasten a noose or gin on the mouth or nose (of a horse, &c.).

def: s.f. A rope or cord used instead of a bridle, a nose-string (syn. bāg-ḍor; nakel).
more: s. f. A cord used instead of a bridle.

def: s.f. Advancing towards an enemy, confronting or assaulting an enemy, assault.
n. f. A noose or gin fastened on a horse's nose. ḍhāṭī chaṛhānā, lagānā, yā denā , v. a. To fasten a noose or gin on the mouth of a horse, etc.
s.f. Binding tight, stretching on the rack; rack, torment, torture; — a noose or gin fastened on a horse's nose; nose-string (of an ox, or camel, &c.; syn. nakel)
subs: or denā, or lagānā (-kī, or -ko), To fasten a noose or gin on the mouth or nose (of a horse, &c.).

def: adj. & s.m. Sturdy, lusty, bulky; bold, overbearing; base, low, ignoble, spurious; — a lusty fellow, a great hulk, &c. (see ḍhaṭīngṛā).
adj. & s.m. Sturdy, burly, &c. ( = dhaṭīṅgar, q.v.); — a big burly fellow, a great hulk of a man.
adj. & s.m. Sturdy, burly, &c. ( = dhaṭīṅgar, q.v.); — a big burly fellow, a great hulk of a man.

n. m. A great big fellow. 1. Apnā pūt, aur kā ḍhaṭīṅgar . Wom. Prov. One's own's a son, another's a big hulking fellow. 2. Ḍhaṭīṅgar kāhe moṭā? lāhā gine na ṭoṭā . Prov. Why is this hulk so fat? He hath no greed, no losses make him pine.
s.f. A rope or cord used instead of a bridle, a nose-string (syn. bāg-ḍor; nakel).

ety: dhat, 'a habit, q.v. + Prk. इओ and अल्लो = S. इकः and अलः
def: adj. & s.m. Having an inveterate habit, addicted to; — one who is addicted to any vice.
more: n. m. One addicted to any vice. aṛīdh'at , adj. Huge; gigantic; stupendous.

ety: dhat, 'a habit, q.v. + Prk. इओ and अल्लो = S. इकः and अलः
def: adj. & s.m. Having an inveterate habit, addicted to; — one who is addicted to any vice.
v. a. To drive out.
s.m. = dhātā, q.v.
s.f. A wet-nurse, a foster-mother, nurse, mother ( = dāʼī); the earth; — emblic myrobalan, Emblica officinalis.
s.f. An epithet of Sarasvatī.
s.m. Emblic myrobalan, the plant Emblica officinalis.

s.m. 1˚ = ḍaṭṭā or ḍāṭ, qq.v.; — 2˚ = ḍhaṭhiyā, q.v. part. & s.m. (f. -ā), Given, granted, presented, made over, assigned; — a common name or title of a man of the Vaiśya caste, and also of some Kāyasths
more: s. m. A plug, cork, bung.
subs: (˚ta+āt˚), adj. & s.m. Self-given; — one who has given himself; a youth or orphan who gives himself to persons disposed to adopt him

def: s. (dakh.) The cloths or dresses with which standards carried by Muhammadans in India, on religious processions, are decked ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
s.f. A rope or cord used instead of a bridle, a nose-string (syn. bāg-ḍor; nakel).
more: s. f. A cord used instead of a bridle.

ety: dhat, 'a habit, q.v. + Prk. इओ and अल्लो = S. इकः and अलः
def: adj. & s.m. Having an inveterate habit, addicted to; — one who is addicted to any vice.

s.m. An element, primary or elementary substance (viz. earth, fire, water, air, atmosphere); constituent element of the body; primary or essential fluid or juice, a secretion; semen; the matter which oozes in a gleet; a humour or affection of the body (as phlegm, wind, and bile); any one of the five qualities or properties of the elements observed by the organs of sense (viz. sound, tangibility, colour, flavour, smell); a primary element of the earth or of mountains, a mineral, fossil, metal, ore; the primary element of words, root, crude form, radical, verbal root
more: n. f. 1. The root letters. 2. The Sanskrit root or crude form of a word. dhātū-biddyā , Metallurgy. dhātū-mārnī , n. f. Borax ( suhāgā ). dhātū-vādī , n. m. A metallurgist; mineralogist; an assayer.
subs: adj. Promoting the elementary juices or secretions
vulg. dhātup, s.m. The alimentary juice, chyle
s.m. 'Chief of minerals,' green sulphate of iron, green vitriol
s.m. An assayer; a miner; — a mineralogist, metallurgist
s.m. 'Friend of metals'; borax (used as a flux)

def: s.m. The thorn-apple (a powerful narcotic), Datura alba, or D. fastuosa (see below)
more: n. m. S. धतूर A narcotic plant and poison, the seeds of which are often given with sweetmeats to cause stupefaction or death. Dhatūrā to nahīṅ khā gayā hai? dhatūriyā , n. m. One who poisons with dhatūrā. dhatūrā denā , v. n. To poison. sādā yā safaid dhatūrā , n. m. Dhatūrā albā . Wat. kālā dhatūrā , n. m. Dhatūrā fastuosā . Wat.
s. m. Name of a plant, Datura fastuosa, or thorn-apple.
subs: (-ko), To administer dhatūrā (to), to drug or poison with dhatūrā

def: s.m. One who drugs or poisons with dhatūrā; — a cheat, knave, impostor.
s.f. The tree Grislea tomentosa, Roxb., or Lythrum fruticosum, Linn., and its wood.
more: n. m. 1. A kind of wood. 2. An iron ring for a wheel. Gāṛī pe dhau chaṛhvā lo .
s. f. A kind of wood. (Lythrum fruticosum: Linn. Grislea tomentosa: Roxb. Pl. Cor. ).

def: s.f. The iron band of a wheel, the strake or tire.

s.m. (dialec.), Deep water, &c. ( = dah, q.v.). s.f. Burning; combustion, conflagration; cauterizing; cautery; — fire; the sensation of burning, internal heat, feverish or morbid heat; inflammation; — ardour; heart-burning, envy, jealousy
subs: (-ko), To apply fire (to), to light (the funeral pile)
v.n. To bear envy, &c., to be envious or jealous; to grudge
s.m. A place where dead bodies are burnt
s.f. The act, or the ceremony, of burning a corpse
s.m. 'Removing heat'; the root of the fragrant grass Andropogon muricatus (which is woven into screens and kept wet for the purpose of tempering the hot winds; see ḵẖas-ḵẖas).

def: s.m. (dialec.), Worry, annoyance, bother.
= dhauṅknā, q.v. v.t. To blow (with bellows, &c.); to breathe upon; — v.n. To blow, to puff, pant.
s.f. = dhauṅknī, q.v. s.f. Blowing, panting, hard breathing; — bellows
subs: (-ko), To suffer from shortness of breath, to be blown; to puff, pant, breathe hard.

ety: S. धाव (rt. धाव)+al = Prk. -अडी = S. अ+ल
def: s.f. A thump, buffet, rap, slap, box
more: n. f. See دهپا Lambā be-ḍaul, ek ghūṅsā, do dhaul. dhaul dhappā, dhaul chhakṛī , n. m. A fight. dhaul lagānā, mārnā, yā jaṛnā , v. a. To thump.
s. f. A thump, a rap, a slap. دهول جڙنا يا لگانا يا مارنا dhaul-jaṛnā or -lagānā or -mārnā, v. a. To thump. دهول لگنا dhaul lagnā, v. n. To suffer loss.
subs: s.f. or dhaul-dhappā, s.m. Thumping and slapping, mutual cuffing, a fight, row
v.n. To get a thump or buffet; to receive a blow or shock, to suffer loss

def: s. m. Thumping and slapping.
s.m. A kind of sugar-cane; the stalk of joʼār (which is sucked as sugar-cane is).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), White, clean, bright; — the colour white; a white ox or bull; any white thing (as milk, &c.); a kind of sugar-cane, &c. ( = dhaul, q.v.)
more: adj. S. धवल Pālī, dhavalo . 1. White. Mūṅjaṛ bān bisāūṅ nāhīṅ, San kī khāṭ bunāūṅgī; Dhaulā gūdaṛ roj bichhāūṅ; Mailā nāṅh bichhāūṅgī . Rus. Wom. song. Mūṅj for my bed I will not buy, On a bed of hempen rope I'll lie, A clean white bedding I will spread, I will not lie on a dirty bed. 2. Any white thing, esp. milk. Dhaulā khā-kar kahīṅ bāhar bhitar mat jā! Wom. dhaulā paṛnā , v. n. To turn pale. dhaulā pan , n. m. Whiteness. dhaulā, dhaul , n. m. The stalk of juār , sucked as sugar cane.
adj. White. دهولاگر dhaulāgir ( धवलगिरी ) s. m. Name of a mountain.
subs: v.n. To turn white or pale; — dhaulā-giri, dhaulā-gir, s.m. Name of one of the highest peaks of the snowy range of the Himālayas ( = dhavala-giri).

ety: dhaulā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Whiteness, cleanness, brightness.
s. f. Whiteness.

ety: dhaul, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To thump, slap, buffet, cuff ( = dhaul mārnā).
more: v. a. To thump, to slap.
v. a. (caus. of دهونا dhonā ) To cause to wash.

ety: dhaulā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Whiteness, &c. ( = dhaulāʼī, q.v.). s.f. Whiteness, cleanness, brightness.
v.t. = dhaulānā, q.v. v.t. To thump, slap, buffet, cuff ( = dhaul mārnā).
more: (from دهول dhaul ) v. a. To thump, to box, to slap, to cuff.

def: conj. Whether.

adj. 1. Multiplication table of 2½ times. 2. A technical term in the multiplication of fractions used in the Native system of Arithmetic and by surveyors in reducing linear measurement to Bighās. Ek ḍhāūṅ ḍhāūṅ, do ḍhāūṅ pāṅch .
adj. & s.m. = ḍhām & ḍhāmā, q.v. s.f. Sound, loud noise or report (as of a drum, &c.); thud
subs: s.f. Noise, tumult, bustle, riot, row
s.m.(f.?), Sound of walking over a hollow place, or of stamping, or of continued beating or thumping, or drumming, &c.; — adv. With a loud noise, with a bang, thump-thump, drum-drum, &c.
s.f. Sound of a drum, &c.; — 2˚ = dham-ā-dham
adv. With a loud noise or report, with a thud, noisily; heavily; suddenly, all at once.

def: s.m. A weight of half a man or twenty seers (ser).
more: A twenty-seer weight. dhaun bhar , Twenty seers in weight.
1. s. m. A weight of 20 ser. 2. Whether.

def: n. m. The multiplication table of 4½ times.
s.f. Thought, consideration, reflection (syn. dhyān).
s.f. Blowing, puffing; breathing, panting; — the asthma.
more: (from دهونکنا q. v.) s. f. 1. Breathing, panting. 2. The asthma.

def: s.f. Bellows ( = dhauṅknī).

ety: dhauṅk˚ = Ap. Prk. धवँक्क(इ), Prk. धमक्के(इ), fr. S. धम (rt.ध्मा+कृ
def: v.t. To blow (with bellows, &c.); to breathe upon; — v.n. To blow, to puff, pant.
more: v. a. S. धमा to blow. To blow the bellows.
v. a. To blow with bellows, &c.

def: s.f. Blowing, panting, hard breathing; — bellows
more: n. f. Bellows. dhauṅknī yā hauṅknī lagnā , v. n. To breathe quickly; to pant. Bachche ko dhauṅknī lagī huī hai .
subs: (-ko), To suffer from shortness of breath, to be blown; to puff, pant, breathe hard.

def: s.f. Noisy demeanour, overbearing conduct, bluster; impetuous assault; threatening, menace; imposition, exaction, extortion; deception, trick, cheating; — s.m. A large kettle-drum
more: n. f. S. ध्वंस 1. ( dhauṅs paṭṭī ) Trick; cheat. Achchhī dhauṅs dī . 2. Exaction; extortion; compulsory payment. Yeh aur dhauṅs āī . 3. A threat; menace. dhauṅs bāṅdhnā , v. a. See kharch bāṅdhnā. dhauṅs denā, dhauṅs kī chalnā , v. a. 1. To trick; cheat; deceive. 2. To hold out a threat. dhauṅs meṅ ānā , v. n. To be tricked, etc. Tumhārī dhauṅs meṅ āne-vāle koī aur hoṅge! dhauṅs'iyā , n. m. A cheat.
subs: s.m. Blustering, bullying, tumult; impetuous assault (of a crowd or body of men)
(-ko), To threaten; to deceive, trick, cheat
(-kī), To be influenced by a threat; — to be taken in, be tricked or cheated.

def: s. m. Blustering, bullying, tumult, the impetuous assault of a crowd or body of men.
s.m. Boldness, daringness, presumption; power, ability; attack, assault; shock, blow, injury, misfortune; — a large keetle-drum
more: n. m. S. दुन्दुस्वन 1. A large kettledrum. 2. ( Slang ). Ability; strength. Uskā kyā dhauṅsā hai jo is kharch kī ṭakkar jhele! dhauṅsā khānā , v. n. To be ill-fated. Kískī māṅ ne dhauṅsā khāyā hai ? Prov. What ill-fated mother bore thee?
subs: v.n. To suffer a shock or blow, to be unfortunate or ill-fated.

def: s.m. One who conducts himself audaciously or overbearingly; the leader of an assault, the leader of a hue and cry or posse comitatus; — an extortioner; a trickster, a cheat.
more: s. m. The leader of a hue and cry or posse comitatus.

def: adj. Rich, wealthy; profuse, lavish, open-handed, unsparing; — stout, strong; bold, brave; brisk, smart, active, sharp; loose, disorderly, licentious; vicious, mischievous, wicked.
more: Wom. adj. 1. Quick; sharp. 1. Khāne meṅ dhauṅtāl hai . 2. Yeh aurat kām meṅ baṛī dhauṅtāl hai . 3. Ek mere bachche ke lete waqt hi behāl ho, Aur to kāmoṅ meṅ tum, annī, baṛīd auṅtāl ho . Wom. Nāznīn. 2. Vicious; mischievous; wicked.
adj. 1. Rich, wealthy. 2. Strong, stout. 3. Bold. 4. Vicious, mischievous.

ety: dhauṅtāl, q.v.+ī = āʼī = Prk.अइआ = S.aff. ता+इका; — and -(adj.) Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.f. Riches; profuseness, lavishness; strength; courage, &c. (see dhauṅtāl); — adj. = dhauṅtāl.
more: s. f. 1. Riches. 2. Strength. 3. Courage. 4. Viciousness, mischievousness.

def: s.m. A large species of dove; — a kind of sugar-cane.
more: s. m. A large kind of dove.

def: adj. White, &c. ( = dhaulā, q.v.). adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), White, clean, bright; — the colour white; a white ox or bull; any white thing (as milk, &c.); a kind of sugar-cane, &c. ( = dhaul, q.v.)
subs: v.n. To turn white or pale; — dhaulā-giri, dhaulā-gir, s.m. Name of one of the highest peaks of the snowy range of the Himālayas ( = dhavala-giri).

def: adj. Of or pertaining to a cheat; cheating, dishonest, roguish, knavish, fraudulent, &c.
part. Washed, cleansed, purified; bleached; polished, bright — dhauta-śila, s.m. 'Bright stone'; crystal, rock-crystal.

To make a long and expeditious march, to make a forced march; to make an expedition, to make a sudden attack (on, -par), to invade.

ety: Prk. धावओ; S. धावकः, rt. धाव्
def: s.m. Running, going expeditiously; a loud and expeditious march, a forced march; an expedition, invasion, overrunning (of a country), incursion, inroad; attack, assault; a rushing or crowding together; a stock, store
more: n. m. S. धावा to run. 1. A long expeditious march. 2. An attack; incursion; a raid. dhāvā k. , v. a. 1. To fall upon; attack; run against. 2. To surprise. dhāvā mārnā , v. a. To make a long quick march, or a great circuit; to make a raid.
n. m. H. dhaulā white. A plant which yields a dye; Grislea tomentosa . Wat.

To make a long and expeditious march, to make a forced march; to make an expedition, to make a sudden attack (on, -par), to invade.

def: s.m. = dhāʼo, q.v. s.m. The plant Grislea tomentosa (used in dyeing).

ety: prob. S. धावकः, rt. धाव 'to run'
def: s.m. A Musalmān caste of pālkī bearers; a member of that caste.
more: s. m. A cast of pālkī bearers, who are Musalmāns.

def: s.m. A man; a husband; possessor, master, lord; — the plant Grislea tomentosa.
adj. Running, going quickly, expeditious, swift.
adj. Cleaning, cleansing, washing.
adj. (f. -ā, or -ī), & s.m. White, dazzling white; handsome, beautiful; — the colour white; — the plant Grislea tomentosa (see dhav; dhau); — name of an inferior rāg or mode in music

def: n. m. Dim. ḍherī , n. f. A heap ( aṭālā , 1.). 1. Sāṅīṅ ke darbār meṅ baṛe baṛe haiṅ ḍher, Apnā dānā bīn le jis meṅ her na pher! Dohā. In God's Courts are supplied of food grains store enough, Pick out what's yours, nor short nor over your bread-stuff! 2. Aur is meṅ kahīṅ mil gaī gar husn kī ḍherī Phir jab tū na kah merī, na maiṅ kuchh kahūṅ terī . Nazīr. And when perchance all charms of beauty heaped you see, I've nought to say to you, nor you to say to me. ḍher rahnā, yā ho jānā , v. n. 1. To become a heap. 2. To fall (a house). 3. To become an earth-heap or corpse. Jitne laṛne gaë the vahīṅ ḍher rahe. ḍher k. , v. a. 1. To accumulate; pile up; store up. 2. To make an earth-heap or clod; to kill. Ek nār haigī baṛī raṅgī, Lāgat hai ḍar dekhat naṅgī, Piyār kare, aur kamar ko pakṛe, Chamak jāë jit ḍher kare . Riddle. Ans. Talvār . A sword. A female very bright, to see her naked doth affright, Clings to your side so fond, each flash makes heaps the dust to bite.
more: adj. Much; abundant; enough. ḍherī , n. f. 1. A sub-division of a Paṭṭī . 2. A share in a joint-tenancy village.

n. m. S. दोण 1. A crow. Kāg paṛhāvat pīṅjrā, paṛh gaë chāroṅ Bed, Samjhāë samjhe nahīṅ, rahe ḍheḍ ke ḍheḍ . Girdhar. The crow taught in the cage, four Veds he learnt quite true, The sense he ne'er could learn, a lout remained all through. 2. A low caste who work in leather and hides and are employed as watchmen and messengers in village establishments. They trace their origin from crows whom they worship. They eat dead animals, clean the skins, and sell them to the Chamārs. ḍheṛī kavvā , n. m. The Nutcracker; Nucifraga hemispila . Found only in the Himalayas within Indian limits.
n. f. The humour in the eye ( chīpaṛ ).

more: n. f. S. दुहिता, Pālī, duhitā, dhīṭā ; Pr. dhīā, dhūdā . A daughter ( beṭī ). 1. Bhāvaj kī bhāvaj to, bīrā, dhī hī janegī, bans ghaṭegā uske bāp kā, Mhārī to bhāvaj, bīrā, pūt janegī, bans baḍhegā mhāre bāp kā . A curse and a blessing. Wom. song. Boy, boy! you have pice buried in your house, your parents rob and fetch it hence! 2. Bhari phāṅṛ le lūṅ, Sitalā, ḍhebuā, rupaëbā, chali bhailūṅ Hājīpur bajār. Ejagatjannī! Sagre bajār ghumī phirī ailū. Sitalā maiyā! ṛauāṅ joge katahūṅ nā chunrī bikāi! Bhoj. Hymn. O Sītala! a fistful of rupees and pice I took to the bazar in Hajipur. You sit with gold and suck the lips of each sweet maid! 2. Baiṭhī thī ḍhig māë ke sundar dūtī achānak jāë sunāi, 'Kā baithī ho mandar sundar meṅ, chal, tohe more Kān bulāī!' 'Tore Kān ko chīnhūṅ, na jānūṅ, baurī bhaī, daruā hai khāī? Āg lage Brij ke basibo ko, jahāṅ pānī meṅ āg lagāëṅ lugāī!' Brij. Holī. The fair close by her mother sat when suddenly One came and said 'come, sit not here, Kān sends for thee!' 'I wot not, know not Kān, or mad or drunk are you? Burn Brij where women water set on fire do!'

def: Farrukh, n. f. 1. A washerwoman; laundress. 2. A washerman's wife. 3. A kind of tern or fish.
s.m. A washerman
more: n. m. S. धावक A washerman. Dhobī kā kuttā; ghar kā, na ghāṭ kā . Prov. Like a washerman's dog; nor of the house nor of the ghāṭ . (From pillar to post). dhobī-pāṭ , n. m. 1. The washerman's board or stone on which he beats the clothes for washing 2 A trick in wrestling, so called because the wrestler throws his opponent with both hands as a washerman beats the clothes.
subs: s.m. A washerman's board or stone (on which he beats the clothes when washing); — a trick in wrestling (by which a wrestler throws his adversary with both hands as a washerman dashes down his clothes on the stone).

ety: dhob, q.v.+ Prk. इणी = S. इनी
def: s.f. A washerman's wife; a washerwoman, laundress
more: s. f. A washer-woman, a washermanʼs wife.
subs: s.f. A wagtail (so called from the bird's frequenting the ghāṭs where dhobīs wash clothes; syn. mamolā).

ety: dhob, q.v.+ Prk. इणी = S. इनी
def: s.f. A washerman's wife; a washerwoman, laundress
subs: s.f. A wagtail (so called from the bird's frequenting the ghāṭs where dhobīs wash clothes; syn. mamolā).

def: s.m. Hole, breach, fissure (in a vessel, or wall, &c.); an earthen vessel with a hole or holes in it, an old, broken vessel; an old, broken, or dilapidated house, or wall.
n. m. 1. An old earthen vessel. 2. (Wom.) A broken or old house ( ṭhīkrā ).

n. m. See tāziyā . 1. The tomb carried about in the Muharram , called tāziā . 2. A house ( ghar ); shop ( dukān ). Ab to ḍhoḍā chal niklā . 3. An empty show. ḍhoḍā banā rakhnā , v. a. To keep up the appearance of one's former state or dignity.
s.m. = ḍhoṛā, ḍhoṛhā, q.v. s.m. A kind of medicine (cf. dhatūrā).
